title: rubus fuscoater var. horripilus
intro: Rubus fuscoater var. horripilus is a variety of the black raspberry plant, known for its dark purple fruits and thorny canes. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus fuscoater var. horripilus include its potential to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus fuscoater var. horripilus typically involve the use of the fruits or leaves in teas, infusions, or as a dietary supplement, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
